protocol.h(3) Library Functions Manual protocol.h(3)

NAME

protocol.h - ascii-chat Client Protocol Handler Interface

SYNOPSIS

Functions


int protocol_start_connection ()
Start protocol connection handling. void protocol_stop_connection ()
Stop protocol connection handling. bool protocol_connection_lost ()
Check if connection has been lost. const acip_client_callbacks_t * protocol_get_acip_callbacks ()
Get ACIP client callbacks for packet dispatch.

Detailed Description

ascii-chat Client Protocol Handler Interface

Defines the protocol handling interface for client-side packet processing and data reception thread management.

The protocol handler manages the data reception thread, which continuously receives packets from the server, decrypts them, and dispatches them to appropriate handlers based on packet type. It also detects connection loss and signals the main thread to initiate reconnection.

Command Types Handled

The client processes these command/packet types from the server:

  • PACKET_TYPE_ASCII_FRAME: Terminal display frame (rendered to stdout)
  • PACKET_TYPE_AUDIO_BATCH: Opus-encoded audio samples (sent to audio playback)
  • PACKET_TYPE_PONG: Keepalive response (recorded for timeout detection)
  • PACKET_TYPE_CLEAR_CONSOLE: Terminal clear command (resets display)
  • PACKET_TYPE_SERVER_STATE: Server state updates (logged for debugging)

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

2025

See also

topic_client_protocol "Protocol Architecture"

Definition in file client/protocol.h.

Function Documentation

bool protocol_connection_lost ()

Check if connection has been lost. Atomically checks whether the data reception thread has detected connection loss. Connection loss is detected by:

  • Socket read error (recv() returns error)
  • Socket closed by server (recv() returns 0)
  • Decryption failure (corrupted stream)
  • Invalid packet magic number (out of sync)

Once loss is detected, this flag remains true until the next successful connection.

Returns

true if connection lost (detected by protocol thread), false otherwise

Note

This is checked by the main thread to trigger reconnection logic. The flag is set atomically by the protocol thread and checked atomically by main.

See also

server_connection_lost "Signal connection loss (called by protocol thread)"

Check if connection has been lost

Returns

true if protocol detected connection loss, false otherwise

Definition at line 1358 of file client/protocol.c.

1358                                 {
1359   return atomic_load_bool(&g_data_thread_exited) || server_connection_is_lost();
1360 }




References atomic_load_bool(), and
    server_connection_is_lost().






const
  acip_client_callbacks_t * protocol_get_acip_callbacks ()


Get ACIP client callbacks for packet dispatch. Returns the packet
    dispatch callbacks structure used to route received packets to appropriate
    handlers. This is primarily used for ACDS signaling packet dispatch and
    WebRTC peer negotiation.




The callbacks structure includes handlers for:


    
  
  • ASCII frame rendering
    • 
  
  • Audio batch processing
    • 
  
  • Server state updates
    • 
  
  • Connection control commands (clear, reset, etc.)
    • 



Returns


Pointer to client callbacks structure (never NULL)


Used by WebRTC sessions to receive and dispatch ACDS signaling
    packets.




Returns pointer to the global callback structure for use by WebRTC
    sessions that need to receive ACDS signaling packets.




Returns


Pointer to client callbacks (never NULL)


Definition at line 957 of file
  client/protocol.c.


957                                                              {
958   return &g_acip_client_callbacks;
959 }










int
  protocol_start_connection ()


Start protocol connection handling. Spawns the data reception
    thread in the global client worker thread pool. The thread begins receiving
    and processing packets from the server. Must be called after the server
    connection is established and the cryptographic handshake is complete.




The thread will:



  
1.

  
Continuously receive packets from the server socket

  
2.

  
Decrypt packets using the negotiated session key

  
3.

  
Dispatch packets to appropriate handlers based on type

  
4.

  
Detect connection loss and signal the main thread




Returns


0 on success, negative on error (thread pool full or
  other resources exhausted)


Note


Must be paired with protocol_stop_connection() to
  properly shut down the thread before reconnection or exit.


See also


protocol_stop_connection "Stop data reception
  thread"



protocol_connection_lost "Check if connection was
    lost"




Start protocol connection handling




Initializes protocol state and starts the data reception thread.
    Must be called after successful server connection establishment.




Returns


0 on success, negative on error


Definition at line 1155 of file
  client/protocol.c.


1155                                 {
1156   log_warn("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] 🟢 PROTOCOL_START: Starting client protocol initialization");
1157 
1158   // Register protocol atomics with named debug registry
1159   static bool protocol_atomics_registered = false;
1160   if (!protocol_atomics_registered) {
1161     NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&g_data_thread_exited, "protocol_data_reception_thread_exit_confirmation", NULL);
1162     NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&g_frames_rendered, "protocol_frames_successfully_rendered", NULL);
1163     protocol_atomics_registered = true;
1164   }
1165 
1166   // Reset protocol state for new connection
1167   g_server_state_initialized = false;
1168   g_last_active_count = 0;
1169   g_should_clear_before_next_frame = false;
1170 
1171   log_info("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] ✅ STATE_RESET: server_initialized=false, active_count=0, clear_flag=false");
1172 
1173   // Reset display state for new connection
1174   display_reset_for_new_connection();
1175 
1176   // Send CLIENT_CAPABILITIES packet FIRST before starting any threads
1177   // Server expects this as the first packet after crypto handshake
1178   log_debug("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] 📤 SENDING_CAPABILITIES: terminal_size negotiation");
1179   asciichat_error_t cap_result = threaded_send_terminal_size_with_auto_detect((int)terminal_get_effective_width(),
1180                                                                               (int)terminal_get_effective_height());
1181   if (cap_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1182     log_error("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] ❌ CAPABILITIES_FAILED: cannot send terminal size");
1183     return -1;
1184   }
1185   log_debug("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] ✅ CAPABILITIES_SENT: terminal_size sent successfully");
1186 
1187   // Send STREAM_START packet with combined stream types BEFORE starting worker threads
1188   // This tells the server what streams to expect before any data arrives
1189   uint32_t stream_types = STREAM_TYPE_VIDEO; // Always have video
1190   if (GET_OPTION(audio_enabled)) {
1191     stream_types |= STREAM_TYPE_AUDIO; // Add audio if enabled
1192   }
1193   log_info("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] 📤 SENDING_STREAM_START: types=0x%x (video%s)", stream_types,
1194            (stream_types & STREAM_TYPE_AUDIO) ? "+audio" : "");
1195   asciichat_error_t stream_result = threaded_send_stream_start_packet(stream_types);
1196   if (stream_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1197     log_error("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] ❌ STREAM_START_FAILED: cannot send stream types");
1198     return -1;
1199   }
1200   log_info("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] ✅ STREAM_START_SENT: stream_types=0x%x, server will send frames", stream_types);
1201 
1202   // Start data reception thread
1203   log_warn("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] 🔄 STARTING_DATA_THREAD: callbacks registered, about to spawn thread");
1204   atomic_store_bool(&g_data_thread_exited, false);
1205   if (thread_pool_spawn(g_client_worker_pool, data_reception_thread_func, NULL, 1, "data_reception") != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1206     log_error("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] ❌ DATA_THREAD_SPAWN_FAILED: cannot start frame receive thread");
1207     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Data reception thread creation failed");
1208     return -1;
1209   }
1210   log_warn("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] ✅ DATA_THREAD_SPAWNED: frame receive thread is now running, waiting for frames...");
1211 
1212   // Start webcam capture thread
1213   log_debug("Starting webcam capture thread...");
1214   if (capture_start_thread() != 0) {
1215     log_error("Failed to start webcam capture thread");
1216     return -1;
1217   }
1218   log_debug("Webcam capture thread started successfully");
1219 
1220   // Initialize audio sender thread BEFORE starting audio capture
1221   // This ensures sender is ready when capture thread starts queueing packets
1222   // Must happen after connection succeeds to prevent deadlock if connection fails
1223   audio_sender_init();
1224 
1225   // Start audio capture thread if audio is enabled
1226   log_debug("Starting audio capture thread...");
1227   if (audio_start_thread() != 0) {
1228     log_error("Failed to start audio capture thread");
1229     return -1;
1230   }
1231   log_debug("Audio capture thread started successfully (or skipped if audio disabled)");
1232 
1233   // Start keepalive/ping thread to prevent server timeout
1234   log_debug("Starting keepalive/ping thread...");
1235   if (keepalive_start_thread() != 0) {
1236     log_error("Failed to start keepalive/ping thread");
1237     return -1;
1238   }
1239   log_debug("Keepalive/ping thread started successfully");
1240 
1241   g_data_thread_created = true;
1242   return 0;
1243 }






References atomic_store_bool(), audio_sender_init(),
    audio_start_thread(), capture_start_thread(),
    display_reset_for_new_connection(), g_client_worker_pool,
    keepalive_start_thread(), log_warn,
    terminal_get_effective_height(),
    terminal_get_effective_width(), thread_pool_spawn(),
    threaded_send_stream_start_packet(), and
    threaded_send_terminal_size_with_auto_detect().






void
  protocol_stop_connection ()


Stop protocol connection handling. Signals the data reception
    thread to exit and waits for it to join. Performs proper cleanup to prevent
    resource leaks and race conditions during reconnection or shutdown.




The function:



  
1.

  
Closes the server socket (interrupts blocking recv() in data thread)

  
2.

  
Sets the global shutdown flag (signals thread to exit)

  
3.

  
Waits for the data reception thread to join (polls for thread exit)




This must be called before reconnecting to prevent socket
    descriptor leaks and to ensure the thread has fully cleaned up before
  reuse.




Note


This function blocks until the data reception thread
  exits. Typical wait time: <100ms (socket closure immediately interrupts
  recv()).


See also


protocol_start_connection "Start data
  reception thread"


Stop protocol connection handling




Gracefully shuts down the data reception thread and cleans up
    protocol state. Safe to call multiple times.


Definition at line 1253 of file
  client/protocol.c.


1253                                 {
1254   // Log final frame count before shutting down (always log for diagnostic purposes)
1255   int final_frame_count = atomic_load_u64(&g_frames_rendered);
1256   log_info("📊 CLIENT SESSION STATS: %d unique frames rendered during connection", final_frame_count);
1257 
1258   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 1. Starting protocol_stop_connection");
1259 
1260   // In snapshot mode, data reception thread was never started, but capture thread may still be running
1261   // Always stop the capture thread to prevent use-after-free when transport is destroyed
1262   if (GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode)) {
1263     log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] Snapshot mode: flushing H.265 encoder before stopping capture thread");
1264     // Flush the encoder to output any buffered frames before exiting
1265     if (g_h265_encoder) {
1266       uint8_t flush_buf[1024];
1267       size_t flush_size = sizeof(flush_buf);
1268       asciichat_error_t flush_result = h265_encoder_flush(g_h265_encoder, flush_buf, &flush_size);
1269       if (flush_result == ASCIICHAT_OK && flush_size > 0) {
1270         log_info("[PROTOCOL_STOP] Flushed H.265 encoder: got %zu bytes of buffered frames", flush_size);
1271         // Send the flushed frame if we got one
1272         acip_transport_t *transport = server_connection_get_transport();
1273         if (transport && server_connection_is_active()) {
1274           uint32_t width = 320, height = 240; // TODO: track actual dimensions
1275           log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] Sending flushed frame: %ux%u, %zu bytes", width, height, flush_size);
1276         }
1277       }
1278     }
1279     log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] Snapshot mode: stopping capture thread before returning");
1280     capture_stop_thread();
1281     return;
1282   }
1283 
1284   // Don't call signal_exit() here - that's for global shutdown only!
1285   // We just want to stop threads for this connection, not exit the entire client
1286 
1287   // Shutdown the socket FIRST to interrupt any blocking network operations in worker threads
1288   // This must happen BEFORE audio_stop_thread() so audio sender thread unblocks from network send
1289   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 2. About to call server_connection_shutdown");
1290   server_connection_shutdown();
1291   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 3. server_connection_shutdown() returned");
1292 
1293   // Signal audio sender thread to exit
1294   // Must happen after socket shutdown so any blocked network calls fail
1295   // Audio sender is created in ALL modes except snapshot mode
1296   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 4. About to call audio_stop_thread");
1297   audio_stop_thread();
1298   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 5. audio_stop_thread() returned");
1299 
1300   // Early return if data thread was never created (e.g., mirror mode)
1301   // In mirror mode, we only need to stop the audio sender (done above)
1302   if (!g_data_thread_created) {
1303     log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 6. Data thread not created, returning");
1304     return;
1305   }
1306 
1307   // Stop keepalive/ping thread - it checks connection status and will exit
1308   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 7. About to call keepalive_stop_thread");
1309   keepalive_stop_thread();
1310   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 8. keepalive_stop_thread() returned");
1311 
1312   // Stop webcam capture thread
1313   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 9. About to call capture_stop_thread");
1314   capture_stop_thread();
1315   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 10. capture_stop_thread() returned");
1316 
1317   // Wait for data reception thread to exit gracefully
1318   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 11. Waiting for data thread to exit");
1319   // Thread checks should_exit() every read cycle (typically <1-5ms), so timeout can be much shorter
1320   int wait_count = 0;
1321   while (wait_count < 5 && !atomic_load_bool(&g_data_thread_exited)) {
1322     platform_sleep_us(10 * US_PER_MS_INT); // 10ms * 5 = 50ms max wait
1323     wait_count++;
1324   }
1325 
1326   if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_data_thread_exited)) {
1327     log_warn("Data thread not responding after 50ms - will be joined by thread pool");
1328   }
1329   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 12. Data thread wait complete");
1330 
1331   // Join all threads in the client worker pool (in stop_id order)
1332   // This handles the data reception thread and (eventually) all other worker threads
1333   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 13. About to call thread_pool_stop_all");
1334   if (g_client_worker_pool) {
1335     asciichat_error_t result = thread_pool_stop_all(g_client_worker_pool);
1336     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1337       log_error("Failed to stop client worker threads");
1338       LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Thread pool stop failed");
1339     }
1340   }
1341   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 14. thread_pool_stop_all() returned");
1342 
1343   g_data_thread_created = false;
1344 
1345 #ifdef DEBUG_THREADS
1346   log_debug("Data reception thread stopped and joined by thread pool");
1347 #endif
1348   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 15. protocol_stop_connection complete");
1349 }






References atomic_load_bool(), atomic_load_u64(),
    audio_stop_thread(), capture_stop_thread(),
    g_client_worker_pool, g_h265_encoder,
    h265_encoder_flush(), keepalive_stop_thread(),
    log_warn, platform_sleep_us(),
    server_connection_get_transport(),
    server_connection_is_active(), server_connection_shutdown(),
    and thread_pool_stop_all().
